notes: v351 — A STOP YOU SET IN THE PORTFOLIO NOW OVERRIDES YOUR RULES (and Undo is back). THE FIX: when you set a 🛑 stop on a holding by hand, it used to get overridden by your trailing-stop rule — if the trailing stop was tighter than your stop, the rule won and could sell the share out from under you (and it even wrote its own value over the one you typed). Now a stop you set in the portfolio is YOURS: the trailing, break-even and max-hold rules leave that holding alone, and it only sells at your stop (or your target). You'll see a gold 🔒 fixed next to a stop you've set so you know it's locked. Clear the stop to hand the holding back to your rules. (Stops set before this update aren't automatically locked — re-enter any you want to fix in place. Buys that pre-fill a stop from your rules, and the 🤖 auto-pilot's own stops, still trail as before.) UNDO IS BACK: I've restored the ↩ Undo buttons and the '🪤 Restore all auto-sold' banner in the portfolio so you can bring back anything the rules already sold — then set a 🔒 stop on it so it can't happen again. Hard-refresh (Ctrl+F5) to load v351. ALSO RECENT — v350 — table redraw no longer freezes. v349 — data labelled 'end-of-day'. v348 — Portfolio in four tabs. (Older notes live in the Owner's Manual changelog.)
